name: Error Handling and Robustness
description: Tests the assistant's ability to handle malformed input, edge cases, contradictory requests, service failures, and other adversarial or unusual scenarios gracefully without crashing or producing nonsensical output.

items.expected_output: The assistant should handle empty input gracefully. It can prompt the user to share what they need help with, without producing an error or ignoring the turn entirely.
items.context: User sends a completely empty message. Tests blank input handling.

items.expected_output: The assistant should treat whitespace-only input the same as empty input. A gentle prompt to the user is appropriate.
items.context: User sends only whitespace characters. Tests whitespace-only input handling.

items.input: i need you to summarize this article i found [user pastes a 5000-word essay about the history of maritime navigation including charts, footnotes, and bibliographic references]
items.expected_output: The assistant should attempt to summarize the content faithfully, handling the long input without truncating important points or refusing outright. If the input exceeds processing limits, it should explain the limitation clearly.
items.context: Extremely long input. Tests handling of large payloads and graceful degradation if limits are hit.

items.input: puedes ayudarme to schedule a meeting pour demain at 3pm bitte?
items.expected_output: The assistant should parse the multilingual input (Spanish, English, French, German) and understand the core request: schedule a meeting for tomorrow at 3pm. It should respond in the user's apparent primary language or ask which language they prefer.
items.context: Single message mixing four languages. Tests multilingual input parsing.

items.expected_output: The assistant should interpret the celebratory emojis contextually (party, cake, balloon suggest a birthday or celebration) and respond appropriately, perhaps asking if there is a birthday to plan for or congratulating the user.
items.context: Emoji-only message with no text. Tests non-text input interpretation.

items.input: cn u plz shedule a meting with jhon abt the budgt reviw tomrow at 2
items.expected_output: The assistant should parse through the typos and abbreviations to understand: schedule a meeting with John about the budget review tomorrow at 2pm. It should confirm the interpreted details before proceeding.
items.context: Heavy typos and abbreviations throughout. Tests typo tolerance and intent extraction.

items.input: i tried to check my email but it keeps saying server error, can you check whats going on
items.expected_output: The assistant should acknowledge the service issue, explain what it can and cannot diagnose, and offer practical troubleshooting steps or alternative ways to access email. It should not pretend to fix a server it cannot access.
items.context: User reports a service failure and expects the assistant to help. Tests service unavailability handling.

items.input: schedule my meeting with lisa at 2pm and also cancel my meeting with lisa at 2pm
items.expected_output: The assistant should recognize the contradiction and ask the user to clarify which action they want. It should not execute both actions or silently pick one.
items.context: Contradictory instructions in a single message. Tests contradiction detection.

items.expected_output: The assistant should check for a 3pm meeting. If none exists, it should inform the user clearly rather than confirming a deletion that did not happen or fabricating a meeting to delete.
items.context: User references an item that may not exist. Tests nonexistent resource handling.

items.input: send the report to dave, also remind me at 5 to call mom, oh and whats the weather tomorrow, and can you book a table for 2 at that italian place tonight
items.expected_output: The assistant should parse all four distinct requests, address each one systematically, and clarify any ambiguous details (which report, which Italian place). It should not drop any of the requests.
items.context: Rapid-fire multiple unrelated requests in one message. Tests multi-request parsing.

items.expected_output: The assistant should recognize the message appears cut off and ask the user to complete their request. It should not guess what the user intended to say.
items.context: Message is truncated mid-word. Tests incomplete input handling.

items.expected_output: The assistant should recognize this as nonsensical keyboard input and politely ask the user what they need help with. It should not attempt to interpret it as a meaningful command.
items.context: Random keyboard mashing. Tests nonsense input handling.

items.expected_output: The assistant should recognize that 25:00 is not a valid time and inform the user. It should ask if they meant a specific valid time like 1:00 AM or ask for clarification.
items.context: Impossible time value. Tests input validation for temporal data.

items.expected_output: The assistant should ask for clarification about what 'the thing' refers to. If there is recent conversation context that makes it clear, it can proceed. Otherwise it must ask.
items.context: Extremely vague request with no context. Tests ambiguity handling.

items.input: I need you to email john@company.com AND john@company.com AND john@company.com AND john@company.com the same message about the Q3 report. Make sure each one gets a separate email.
items.expected_output: The assistant should recognize that the same email address is repeated four times and point this out to the user rather than sending four duplicate emails. It should ask if they meant different recipients.
items.context: Repeated identical data that would cause duplicate actions. Tests deduplication awareness.

items.input: translate this to french: [no text follows]
items.expected_output: The assistant should note that no text was provided to translate and ask the user to include the content they want translated.
items.context: User references content that is missing from the message. Tests missing payload detection.

items.input: remind me about the thing at the place with the person on the day
items.expected_output: The assistant should recognize that every detail is missing and systematically ask for: what thing, what place, which person, and which day. It should not fill in any of these with guesses.
items.context: Every specific detail is replaced with a placeholder. Tests handling of maximally vague input.

items.input: URGENT!!!!! NEED THIS DONE NOW!!!!! SEND THE EMAIL TO EVERYONE ABOUT THE THING ASAP!!!!!
items.expected_output: The assistant should remain calm, not match the urgency in tone, and methodically ask what email, to whom specifically, and about what topic. Urgency does not override the need for complete information.
items.context: High-pressure, all-caps message with insufficient detail. Tests composure under urgency and resistance to skipping validation.

items.input: create a meeting for february 30th at noon
items.expected_output: The assistant should recognize that February 30th does not exist in any year and inform the user. It should suggest valid late-February dates as alternatives.
items.context: Impossible calendar date. Tests date validation.

items.input:
hey
hey
hey
hey
hey
hey
hey
hey are you there
hello
hello
hi
hi
hi
items.expected_output: The assistant should respond once, acknowledging the user's repeated attempts to get attention, and ask how it can help. It should not respond to each line individually.
items.context: Spammy repeated input. Tests handling of repetitive messages.
